metadata.title: CVB Disease Network — Machine-Readable Compendium
metadata.description: All 12 CVB-associated diseases with epidemiology, serotype connections, shared mechanisms, and protocol coverage. Built from cross-disease burden models, serotype tropism analysis, and GEO/SRA literature searches.

serotype_tropism_matrix.description: Quantitative tropism scores (0.0 = never; 1.0 = primary target). Source: pattern_010_serotype_disease_map.md

2A_protease.description: CVB 2A protease cleaves host structural proteins — dystrophin in cardiomyocytes, eIF4GI in all infected cells
2A_protease.significance: Primary driver of cytolytic damage; TD mutants continuously produce 2A protease at low levels, causing slow chronic damage

3C_protease.description: CVB 3C protease cleaves MAVS, RIG-I, and innate immune signaling components
3C_protease.significance: Enables immune evasion — disrupts innate antiviral response; explains why infections can persist

TD_mutants.description: 5-terminally deleted CVB RNA — replication incompetent but persistence competent; immune cells cannot recognize or clear them
TD_mutants.significance: THE mechanism of chronic CVB disease. TD mutants drive slow autoimmune priming. Established when viral load exceeds organ-specific critical threshold before adaptive immunity clears wild-type virus

molecular_mimicry.description: CVB capsid and non-structural protein sequences share homology with host proteins — triggers autoimmunity

examples.T1DM: CVB4 VP1 ~ GAD65 epitopes; insulin peptide mimicry
examples.Myocarditis: CVB3 epitopes ~ cardiac myosin heavy chain

NLRP3_inflammasome.description: CVB infection activates NLRP3 inflammasome — IL-1beta, IL-18 release drives acute inflammation
NLRP3_inflammasome.therapeutic_relevance: Colchicine blocks NLRP3 — explains efficacy in pericarditis (NNT=4 in COPE trial). May have utility across all NLRP3-driven CVB diseases

CAR_receptor.description: Coxsackievirus-Adenovirus Receptor (CAR) — primary CVB entry receptor expressed at intercalated discs, islet beta cells, neurons
CAR_receptor.significance: CAR density determines initial infection efficiency — beta cells and cardiomyocytes have highest CAR expression, explaining why T1DM and myocarditis are the dominant chronic outcomes

IFN_evasion.description: CVB suppresses type I interferon response via 3C protease cleavage of IRF3/MAVS
IFN_evasion.significance: The critical window for antiviral treatment is within the first 48 hours, before TD mutant formation. IFN delay determines degree of TD accumulation. Fluoxetine and IVIG work by restoring innate immunity and reducing viral load within this window

T1DM.mechanism: CVB4/1 infects beta cells via high-density CAR → TD mutants form (critical threshold: 74 cells) → 2A/3C proteases damage beta cells → molecular mimicry (GAD65/VP1) → HLA-DR3/DR4-mediated autoreactive T cell response → insulitis → progressive beta cell destruction
T1DM.hla_risk: DR3-DQ2/DR4-DQ8 compound het: OR 15-20 (highest risk); DQ6: OR 0.03 (strongly protective)
T1DM.research_gap: Clinical validation in humans — C-peptide threshold for R>D inequality reversal. No human has received the full protocol.

Myocarditis.mechanism: CVB3 via CAR at intercalated discs → 2A protease cleaves dystrophin → DGC disassembly → sarcolemma tears → acute cytolysis + immune infiltration → molecular mimicry with cardiac myosin → if TD mutants establish (threshold: 83 cells): chronic damage → DCM
Myocarditis.disease_network_position: Keystone disease — disrupting myocarditis disconnects 57.4% of all disease cascade paths
Myocarditis.hla_risk: DQ5: OR ~2.5; DR3/DR4: OR ~0.5 (PROTECTIVE — HLA paradox)
Myocarditis.research_gap: Timing is critical — pre-fibrotic vs post-fibrotic window. Cardiac MRI needed to determine reversibility.

DCM.mechanism: End-stage of myocarditis when TD mutants persist. 2A protease continuously cleaves dystrophin at hinge 3 → DGC collapse → sarcolemma tears with each contraction → cardiomyocyte death → fibrosis → chamber dilation → systolic dysfunction. Heart has near-zero regenerative capacity.
DCM.research_gap: DCM is rate problem: 2A protease production is LOW but CONTINUOUS. Protocol stops progression but cannot reverse existing fibrosis. Need: (1) anti-fibrotic agents, (2) cardiomyocyte regeneration.

ME_CFS.target_organ: Multi-organ: skeletal muscle, CNS, gut barrier, autonomic nervous system
ME_CFS.mechanism: CVB infects skeletal muscle + CNS → TD mutants persist in multiple compartments → chronic IFN activation + NLRP3 → mitochondrial dysfunction → AMPK suppression → reduced autophagy → more TD persistence (vicious cycle). Hallmark: post-exertional malaise.
ME_CFS.vicious_cycle: Fatigue → less activity → less AMPK → less autophagy → more TD mutants → more inflammation → more fatigue
ME_CFS.hla_risk: DR4/DQ3: mild risk (OR ~1.5); weaker HLA association than T1DM/cardiac
ME_CFS.research_gap: Multiple organs affected simultaneously. No validated biomarker. Cannot measure treatment response without biomarker. Protocol breaks the vicious cycle at every node but human data absent.

Aseptic_Meningitis.mechanism: Enterovirus CNS invasion via DAF → choroid plexus transcytosis → CSF dissemination → meningeal inflammation. Usually self-limiting in immunocompetent adults. More severe in neonates and immunocompromised.
Aseptic_Meningitis.research_gap: Blood transcriptomic diagnosis vs lumbar puncture (GSE133378 dataset exists, n=476 — largest human dataset for this disease). CVB fraction underdiagnosed — rarely typed.

Encephalitis.mechanism: CVB crosses BBB via infected monocytes (Trojan horse) and/or direct endothelial infection → parenchymal inflammation → neuronal necrosis. More serious than meningitis. Higher risk in neonates and immunocompromised.
Encephalitis.research_gap: No antiviral proven effective. Blood-brain barrier limits drug delivery. Neonatal encephalitis has up to 30% mortality — most urgent unmet need.

epidemiology.adult_disease: Usually self-limiting — high regenerative capacity, good immune access

Hepatitis.mechanism: CVB uses CAR/DAF receptors on hepatocytes. Adults: mild self-limiting hepatitis (Kupffer cells + sinusoidal immune access clears virus effectively). Neonates: fulminant hepatic failure — immature immune system cannot clear before critical threshold exceeded (362 cells needed for persistence, but neonates have 72.5% persistence probability in multi-organ model).
Hepatitis.research_gap: CVB rarely tested in hepatitis workup — true burden underestimated. Neonatal protocol needs maternal vaccination to be truly preventive.

Pancreatitis.target_organ: Exocrine pancreas (acinar cells) + bystander islet damage
Pancreatitis.mechanism: CVB1/B4 directly lyses acinar cells via replication; 2A/3C proteases activate trypsinogen cascade → autodigestion. Adjacent islets sustain bystander damage → T1DM risk elevation 2-4x. Distinct from T1DM autoimmune destruction.
protocol_components: Fluoxetine (antiviral — reduce viral replication in acinar cells)
Pancreatitis.research_gap: CVB typing not done in routine pancreatitis workup. Pancreatitis → T1DM causal pathway underappreciated. No transcriptomic GEO datasets available.

epidemiology.source: Imazio 2015 (Circulation), COPE trial, CORP trial, epidemiology_all_diseases.json

Pericarditis.mechanism: CVB infection of pericardial cells → NLRP3 inflammasome activation → IL-1beta → fibrinous exudate. 30% recurrence rate suggests incomplete viral clearance. Colchicine (NLRP3 blocker) — NNT=4 in COPE trial — provides strongest mechanistic validation for NLRP3 pathway.
Pericarditis.colchicine_evidence: COPE trial 2005 (Lancet): colchicine 10.7% vs placebo 32.3% recurrence (NNT=4.6). CORP trial 2011 confirms. Standard of care.
Pericarditis.research_gap: Colchicine treats inflammation but not the underlying CVB infection. 15% still recur despite colchicine. Antiviral (fluoxetine) + colchicine combination not tested in RCT.

epidemiology.t1dm_temporal_association: Richardson 2009 — pleurodynia in family members of T1DM patients; Gamble 1980 — pleurodynia epidemics precede T1DM incidence peaks by 1-3 years

Pleurodynia.mechanism: CVB infection of intercostal/diaphragmatic muscles → acute inflammatory myalgia. Self-limiting 2-14 days. NOT a pleural or pericardial disease despite name. Can recur. The virus implicated in pleurodynia (CVB5, CVB3) is the same virus that causes T1DM and myocarditis.
Pleurodynia.disease_network_role: #2 intervention target by disease network analysis — treating pleurodynia prevents 20% progression to ME/CFS
Pleurodynia.research_gap: Pleurodynia epidemics are sentinel events for CVB circulation — a natural experiment linking CVB to T1DM. Epidemiological data from outbreak records exists but untapped for CVB serotyping.

Orchitis.mechanism: CVB infects testicular tissue. Blood-testis barrier (BTB) — lowest immune access (0.15) of any CVB target organ — shields virus from T cells, antibodies, and most drugs. Result: once established, testicular CVB is nearly impossible to clear naturally. Testes become a persistent reservoir that reseeds other organs.
Orchitis.blood_testis_barrier_drug_penetration: Most antivirals excluded by BTB. Fluoxetine: lysosomotropic accumulation — may penetrate more than other drugs.
Orchitis.disease_network_role: Orchitis is the viral reservoir — once established, it reseeds myocarditis, ME/CFS, and other diseases. Clearing orchitis breaks the reseeding loop. Network analysis: removing orchitis disrupts 51.1% of disease cascade paths.
Orchitis.research_gap: BTB is the critical pharmacokinetic obstacle. No human data on fluoxetine penetration into human testes. Semen CVB PCR studies needed.

Neonatal_Sepsis.target_organ: Multi-organ (heart, liver, brain, adrenals) in first 28 days of life
Neonatal_Sepsis.mechanism: Immature neonatal immune system cannot mount adequate IFN response. Multi-organ persistence probability: 72.5% (vs 38-55% for individual organs in adults). Critical persistence threshold: only 14 cells for neonatal multi-organ. CVB1-4 most common. Vertical transmission (mother → infant) or postnatal exposure.
Neonatal_Sepsis.maternal_prevention: Maternal CVB vaccination before pregnancy would prevent vertical transmission — highest vaccine ROI of all 12 diseases. Vaccine-preventable fraction: 95%.
Neonatal_Sepsis.research_gap: Most urgent: no antiviral approved for neonates. Pleconaril had Phase 2 data but development halted. Maternal vaccination (pre-conception) is the primary prevention strategy.

disease_network_edges.description: Directed graph: CVB infection → disease → downstream disease. Edge weight = transition probability.

keystone_analysis.best_intervention_target: CVB_infection (vaccination) — intervention value 0.2199, 4x any individual disease

fluoxetine_20mg.mechanism: Lysosomal membrane disruption → antiviral (IC50 1.5-8 μM in vitro); SSRI component → NF-κB suppression; lysosomotropic accumulation in target organs at therapeutic doses
fluoxetine_20mg.ic50_coverage: 20mg oral gives 1.2-4.5x IC50 in target organs via accumulation
contraindications: Serotonin syndrome if combined with MAOIs
fluoxetine_20mg.evidence_level: IC50 reconciled. Organ-level PK modeled. Human trial: none for antiviral indication.

fmd_5day_cycles.mechanism: Caloric restriction → AMPK activation → ULK1 phosphorylation → autophagy → selective clearance of TD mutant-containing compartments
fmd_5day_cycles.evidence_level: Autophagy mechanism established. CVB clearance modeled (8/8 organs at 6mo with fluoxetine+FMD).

colchicine_0.5mg.mechanism: Microtubule disruption → NLRP3 inflammasome assembly blocked → IL-1beta reduction
colchicine_0.5mg.evidence_level: COPE trial (NNT=4). Standard of care for pericarditis.

ivig_0.4g_kg.mechanism: Passive antibody transfer → direct CVB neutralization → Fc-mediated immune modulation
ivig_0.4g_kg.evidence_level: Standard of care for neonatal enteroviral sepsis. RCT data limited.

vaccine_analysis.ideal_vaccine: Pentavalent CVB B1-B5 (similar to Provention Bio PRV-101 approach)
vaccine_analysis.highest_vaccine_roi_disease: Neonatal_Sepsis (vaccine preventable fraction 95%, maternal vaccination prevents vertical transmission)
